name: Srivastava et al., 'Dropout', JMLR 2014

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'Dropout (Srivastava et al., 2014)'. TOP region: TWO equal-width side-by-side inline-SVG network diagrams sharing the same top and bottom edge - LEFT captioned 'Standard net' (a 3-4-4-2 fully-connected MLP with every edge drawn) and RIGHT captioned 'With dropout' (the same net with about half the hidden units greyed out / crossed and their edges removed). BELOW, a full-width row of THREE equal-width result cards with uniform gaps: 'MNIST error 1.35% -> 1.05%', 'Street View SVHN improved', 'TIMIT phone error reduced'. A dense one-paragraph caption beneath ('Randomly drop units (with prob p) during training to prevent co-adaptation; at test time use all units with weights scaled by p. Equivalent to training an ensemble of 2^n thinned networks sharing weights.'). Footnote at the bottom. ALSO add an equations row: 'train: y = f(D * x), D~Bernoulli(p)' and 'test: y = f(p * x)', plus the note 'ensemble of 2^n thinned nets', with real sub/superscripts.
requirements: The two network diagrams are equal width and share the same top and bottom edge
Both networks have the same node-grid geometry; corresponding layers are at the same vertical positions
In the right diagram the dropped units are visually distinct (greyed/crossed) and their edges removed
No node label or edge overlaps another node's label
The three result cards are equal width with uniform gaps and aligned top/bottom edges
The dense caption paragraph is fully visible (not clipped or overflowing its container)
No element overlaps any other element on the slide
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and overlaps nothing
Every text element is at least 12px
Both train/test dropout equations render correctly (Bernoulli(p), 2^n superscript) and are fully visible, not clipped

ss_html:
Dropout (Srivastava et al., 2014)
Standard net
With dropout
MNIST error
1.35% → 1.05%
Street View SVHN
improved
TIMIT phone error
reduced
train: y = f(D ⊙ x), D~Bernoulli(p) test: y = f(p · x) ensemble of 2n thinned nets
Randomly drop units (with probability p) during training to prevent co-adaptation; at test time use all units with weights scaled by p. Equivalent to training an ensemble of 2n thinned networks sharing weights. This regularization technique forces the network to not rely on specific feature detectors, making it more robust and reducing overfitting by preventing complex co-adaptations on training data.
Srivastava, N., Hinton, G., Krizhevsky, A., Sutskever, I., & Salakhutdinov, R. (2014). Dropout: A simple way to prevent neural networks from overfitting.
rv_html:
Dropout (Srivastava et al., 2014)

Standard net

With dropout

train: y = f(D ⊙ x), D~Bernoulli(p)
test: y = f(p · x)
ensemble of 2n thinned nets
MNIST
1.35% → 1.05%
Street View SVHN
improved
TIMIT phone
error reduced

Randomly drop units (with probability p) during training to prevent co-adaptation; at test time use all units with weights scaled by p. Equivalent to training an ensemble of 2n thinned networks sharing weights. This regularization technique forces the network to not rely on specific feature detectors, making it more robust and reducing overfitting by preventing complex co-adaptations on training data.

Srivastava, N., Hinton, G., Krizhevsky, A., Sutskever, I., & Salakhutdinov, R. (2014). Dropout: A simple way to prevent neural networks from overfitting. JMLR, 15(1), 1929-1958.

  2. [MAJOR] The bottom edges of the two network diagrams are not aligned - the right diagram sits slightly higher (at top-center)
  3. [MAJOR] The three result cards at the bottom have unequal widths - the middle card (SVHN) appears wider than the outer two (at bottom-center)

trace.output:
The issues are: 1. Two network diagrams have different widths - need to ensure equal width 2. Bottom edges of diagrams not aligned 3. Three result cards have unequal widths 4. Gaps between result cards not uniform Let me analyze the current code: For the network diagrams: - They're in a flex container with `flex:1` on each, and `gap:22px` - The issue might be that the SVGs inside have different effective rendering - But actually, `flex: 1 1 0` with `min-width: 0` should force equal sizing by preventing content from expanding the containers I'll apply the same fix to both the network diagrams and result cards - using `flex: 1 1 0` and `min-width: 0` to ensure they divide the available space equally regardless of their internal content. Setting explicit pixel widths based on the container dimensions: diagrams get 905px each and cards get 596px each, using `flex: 0 0` to prevent flex from shrinking them. Actually, I should simplify this with `flex: 1 1 0%` and `min-width: 0` to force equal distribution across the flex items regardless of content. Looking at the issues, the root cause is that `flex:1` allows content to influence the flex-item widths. The fix is to add `flex: 1 1 0%; min-width: 0;` (zero base size) on every flex child in both rows so content can never stretch any item beyond its equal share. ```html Dropout (Srivastava et al., 2014)
